intro.uz: Hayajon — baxtning eng amaliy sinonimi. Tim Ferrissning “The 4-Hour Work Week” kitobidan olingan bu darsda AJ maqsad qoʻyishni oʻrgatadi: maqsad zerikarli emas, hayajonlantiradigan boʻlsin. Bu haftaning ikki grammatikasi: savollar, bilvosita savollar va soʻroq qoʻshimchasi (savol berish); hamda feʼl qoliplari — love doing / want to do / look forward to -ing. Brad haqidagi POV hikoyada ikkalasini ham eshitasiz.
intro.en: Excitement — the most practical synonym for happiness. Built on a section of Tim Ferriss's “The 4-Hour Work Week,” this lesson teaches you to choose goals that excite you, not bore you. This week's two grammar topics: questions, indirect questions and question tags (how to ask); and verb patterns — love doing / want to do / look forward to -ing. You'll hear both in the POV story about Brad.

canDo.uz: Dars oxirida ishtiyoq haqida muloyim, bilvosita savol bera olasiz (“Can you tell me what excites you?”) va soʻroq qoʻshimchasidan foydalana olasiz (“You love this, don't you?”), hamda feʼl qoliplarini toʻgʻri qoʻllab oʻz maqsadlaringizni tasvirlab bera olasiz (“I love learning… I want to speak… I'm looking forward to…”).
canDo.en: By the end you can ask a polite indirect question about excitement (“Can you tell me what excites you?”) and use question tags (“You love this, don't you?”), and describe your goals with correct verb patterns (“I love learning… I want to speak… I'm looking forward to…”).

grammar.titleUz: Savollar, bilvosita savollar va soʻroq qoʻshimchasi
grammar.bandLifter: IELTS Speaking — ask polite, well-formed questions and use natural tags
grammar.cefrCanDo: B2: I can ask polite indirect questions and add question tags correctly
grammar.contrastUz: Oʻzbek tilida savolni ohang yoki “-mi” bilan yasaymiz va soʻz tartibi oʻzgarmaydi (“pul xohlaysanmi?”). Ingliz tili esa yordamchi feʼlni (do/does/is) oldinga chiqaradi: “Do you want money?”. Bilvosita savolda esa u yana ega ortiga qaytadi: “Can you tell me where the store is?”. Soʻroq qoʻshimchasi (“…, don't you?”) — oʻzbekcha “…, shundaymi?”.
grammar.errorFixUz: ❌ Can you tell me where is the bank? You love this, no? ✅ Can you tell me where the bank is? You love this, don't you? Bilvosita savolda oddiy jumla tartibi (do/does yoʻq), tag esa ijobiy jumladan keyin inkor (don't you) boʻladi.

examples.en: Can you tell me what you really want?
You love this idea, don't you?

exercises.hintUz: bilvosita savolda oddiy jumla tartibi (ega + feʼl): the station is
ijobiy jumla → inkor tag; “love” asosiy feʼl → don't you

exercises.prompt: What ___ she want for her birthday?
exercises.hintUz: he/she/it uchun savol → does; keyin feʼl sof qoladi (want)

exercises.promptUz: Doʻstingizdan ishtiyoqi haqida ovoz chiqarib soʻrang: bitta bilvosita savol (“Can you tell me what excites you?”) va bitta tagli jumla (“You love …, don't you?”).
exercises.hintUz: bilvosita savolda oddiy tartib; tag ijobiy jumladan keyin inkor boʻladi

grammar.titleUz: Feʼl qoliplari: love doing / want to do / look forward to -ing
grammar.titleEn: Verb patterns: love doing / want to do / look forward to -ing
grammar.bandLifter: IELTS Speaking — link verbs smoothly (loving, wanting, looking forward to)
grammar.cefrCanDo: B2: I can choose the gerund or infinitive after common verbs without error
grammar.contrastUz: Oʻzbekchada “oʻrganishni yaxshi koʻraman” va “gapirmoqchiman” — ikkalasi ham “-ish/-moq” bilan tugaydi. Ingliz tili esa ikkiga ajratadi: baʼzi feʼl -ing talab qiladi (love learning), baʼzisi to + feʼl (want to speak). “look forward to” dagi “to” — predlog, shuning uchun undan keyin -ing keladi (looking forward to speaking).
grammar.errorFixUz: ❌ I want speaking. I'm looking forward to speak. ✅ I want to speak. I'm looking forward to speaking. want → to + feʼl; look forward to → -ing (bu yerda “to” — predlog).

examples.en: I love learning English and I want to speak it well.
examples.uz: Ingliz tili oʻrganishni yaxshi koʻraman va uni yaxshi gapirmoqchiman.

examples.en: I'm looking forward to starting my new goal.
examples.uz: Yangi maqsadimni boshlashni intiqlik bilan kutyapman.

exercises.prompt: I'm looking forward to ___ you soon.
exercises.hintUz: look forward TO — bu “to” predlog, keyin -ing keladi: meeting

exercises.promptUz: Oʻz maqsadingiz haqida rost gap ayting (ovoz chiqarib): “___ing is exciting. I want to … and I can't wait to … .”
exercises.hintUz: jumla boshida gerund (ega); want/can't wait → to + feʼl

vocab.uz: aniqlik, tafsilotlilik (juda aniq va batafsil boʻlish)
vocab.defEn: the quality of being specific, detailed and exact
vocab.example: It's a matter of specificity — say the exact amount.

vocab.defEn: not exact or clear; too general
vocab.example: “What do you want?” is too imprecise a question.

vocab.defEn: to be destined or certain to end in something
vocab.example: That vague question is fated for confusion.

vocab.defEn: useful and important enough to be worth the effort
vocab.example: He had a worthwhile purpose for needing the money.

vocab.defEn: not clear; open to more than one meaning
please be patient with me for a moment
vocab.example: Bear with me while I think about the answer.

vocab.defEn: a total lack of interest, feeling or concern
vocab.example: The opposite of love is not hate but indifference.

vocab.defEn: a word with the same or a very similar meaning
vocab.example: Excitement is a synonym for happiness.

vocab.uz: cheksiz baxt, samimiy huzur (oddiy baxtdan kuchliroq)
vocab.example: Many people tell you to follow your bliss.

vocab.uz: ishtiyoqingiz ortidan bormoq, sevgan ishingizni qilmoq
vocab.defEn: to do what excites and inspires you most
vocab.example: People suggest that you follow your passion.

warmup.uz: Suhbatda bir yigit hayajon bilan oʻz rok guruhini tuzayotganini aytadi — u nogʻora chalishni oʻrganib, endi qoʻshiqchi qidiryapti. Bu ham darsning mavzusiga mos: ishtiyoq odamni harakatga soladi. Sizningcha, uning rejasi jiddiymi yoki shunchaki hayajonmi?
warmup.en: In this dialogue an excited guy says he's forming a rock band — he's learned the drums and now needs a singer. It fits the theme: excitement drives people to act. Do you think his plan is serious, or just excitement?

keyVocab.defEn: a device that is used to make music
very many times; an exaggeration for emphasis
to create or organize a group
to take part in a trial performance to get a place
a device that makes sound louder
the low, deep-sounding instrument in a band
we are ready; everything is prepared

quiz.q: In 1950, Bertie the Brain was one of the first computer games. How tall was this computer?
quiz.qUz: 1950-yilda Bertie the Brain birinchi kompyuter oʻyinlaridan biri edi. Bu kompyuter qanchalik baland edi?
quiz.explanationUz: Toʻgʻri javob: b) taxminan toʻrt metr. Catherine oʻn metr dedi va xato qildi. Bertie the Brain taxminan toʻrt metr balandlikda edi — hozir esa oʻyinlarni kaftda ushlab oʻynaymiz!

vocab.uz: kibersport (koʻp oʻyinchili kompyuter oʻyinlari boʻyicha musobaqalar)
vocab.defEn: multi-player video game competitions which people pay to watch
playing against other people to win

vocab.uz: biror narsaga qiziqib qolmoq, berilib ketmoq
vocab.defEn: to become really interested in something

vocab.uz: professional (pul evaziga, yuqori darajada ish qiladigan)
vocab.defEn: someone who is paid to do something because they are very good at it
an area of business made up of many different parts
the world of a particular activity — its events, people and competitions

funEnglish.title: Stop searching for your passion | Terri Trespicio | TEDxKC

speakingPrompt.en: What excites you most in life right now — and what's one exciting goal you're working towards? Ask a friend too: “Can you tell me what excites you? You have a big goal, don't you?”
speakingPrompt.uz: Taxminan 60 soniya gapiring. Bu haftaning ikki grammatikasidan foydalaning: savollar va soʻroq qoʻshimchasi (Can you tell me…? …, don't you?) hamda feʼl qoliplari (I love …ing, I want to …, I'm looking forward to …ing).
